human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for arts-and-entertainment creative-operations specificity, consumer-protection and accessibility boundaries, high-volume execution posture, completion-evidence discipline, source-conflict handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to campaign intake, creative production coordination, approval routing, deliverable handoff, exception handling, and completion evidence capture for arts, entertainment, and recreation workflows.

Authority sources cover FTC fee and advertising guidance, DOJ ADA ticket-sales guidance, CFPB refund and charge-dispute guidance, IAB and Ad-ID creative-operating references, FTC BOTS, PCI DSS, and CISA venue-security guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, pricing and refund refusal, rework exceptions, safety and fraud escalation, completion-evidence refusal, terminology fidelity, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence handling, source conflict, high-volume intake, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/arts-entertainment-and-recreation/creative-operations-specialist/ so the lane stays distinct from guest services, front office, pricing or revenue, fraud or loss-prevention, and operations ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around price disclosure, refund promises, accessibility language, fraud signals, venue security, and completion evidence.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated creative operations specialist for evidence-backed campaign execution and policy-aware escalation in arts, entertainment, and recreation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
